This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |Caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |other_designation designation = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (formerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== Burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== Burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}

== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |Caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |other_other_designation other_designation = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== Burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== Burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |Caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |other_designation other_designation = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:aa]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:aa]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |Caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |other_designation other_designation = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and Structure ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |other_designation other_designation = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.

Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}

== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=draft}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=draft}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
