So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=draft}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=draft}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}{{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Deceased = |excavations = {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=draft}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡茶臼山古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=残存する後円部の断面 |所在地=[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]翠松園1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳（前方部滅失） |規模=全長63m |築造年代=6世紀中頃 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=土師器、須恵器、武具、馬具、装身具 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}
'''小幡茶臼山古墳'''（おばたちゃうすやまこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''茶臼山古墳'''と呼ぶ。
== 概要 == 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在した「小幡古墳群」の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。私有地であったこともあって[[1960年代]]には前方部（写真左方向に向かって存在した）が土取りと[[愛知県立緑丘商業高等学校]]（現在の愛知県立緑丘高等学校）への通学路開削で失われており、後円部の一部が残されているものの近年も建物が建てられるなど開発が進んだため、墳丘はかつての姿を留めておらず一見すると単なる崖にしか見えない。なお、[[大正]]時代に[[盗掘]]された記録が残されている。
== 規模と構造 == [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）の測量調査では全長63メートル、後円部径40メートル、前方部幅35メートルなどとされた。[[1988年]]（昭和63年）から翌[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）にかけての発掘調査時の残存部再測量では後円部径32メートル、周濠幅6メートルなどの数値が示されている。また、この調査時に後円部の南東側で[[横穴式石室]]と複数の出土品が発掘され、これによって古墳の主軸が東方55°を向いていることや、くびれ部南側に[[造り出し]]が残存していたことも確認された。
== 主体部 == 左片袖式横穴式石室で、全長約8.2メートル、玄室長約4.8メートル。玄室床面の一部が残存しており、5〜10cmの円礫が敷かれていた。また、閉塞部に加工された石材が確認されるなど、追葬が行なわれた可能性もあるとされ、排水溝と思われる溝も確認されている。主体部は発掘調査後に滅失した。
== 出土品 == 出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。なお、[[大正]]時代にあった盗掘の際に「金ぴかもの」が見つかったという話が伝わっているが詳細は不明である。
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[土師器]] * [[須恵器]] * [[武具]] ** [[鉄刀]] ** [[鉄鏃]] ** [[刀子]] ** [[挂甲]]小札 * [[馬具]] ** 辻金具 ** 馬鈴 ** [[鐙]] * [[装身具]] ** [[金環]] ** 銀環 ** [[玉]] }}
== 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、[[1963年]] * 『守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、[[名古屋市教育委員会]]発行、[[1990年]] * 『愛知県史 資料編3 古墳』- 愛知県史偏さん委員会 編、愛知県発行、[[2004年]] * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会 編、名古屋市発行、[[2008年]]
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[小幡長塚古墳]] - 小幡古墳群の1基。徒歩10分弱の距離に在る。
== アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩で約15分。 * [[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩で約15分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

== 主体部 == 左片袖式横穴式石室で、全長約8.2メートル、玄室長約4.8メートル。玄室床面の一部が残存しており、5〜10cmの円礫が敷かれていた。また、閉塞部に加工された石材が確認されるなど、追葬が行なわれた可能性もあるとされ、排水溝と思われる溝も確認されている。主体部は発掘調査後に滅失した。
== 出土品 == 出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。なお、[[大正]]時代にあった盗掘の際に「金ぴかもの」が見つかったという話が伝わっているが詳細は不明である。
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[土師器]] * [[須恵器]] * [[武具]] ** [[鉄刀]] ** [[鉄鏃]] ** [[刀子]] ** [[挂甲]]小札 * [[馬具]] ** 辻金具 ** 馬鈴 ** [[鐙]] * [[装身具]] ** [[金環]] ** 銀環 ** [[玉]] }}
== 参考文献 == * 『守山の古墳』- 守山市教育委員会、[[1963年]] * 『守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、[[名古屋市教育委員会]]発行、[[1990年]] * 『愛知県史 資料編3 古墳』- 愛知県史偏さん委員会 編、愛知県発行、[[2004年]] * 『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』- 新修名古屋市史史料編編集委員会 編、名古屋市発行、[[2008年]]
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[小幡長塚古墳]] - 小幡古墳群の1基。徒歩10分弱の距離に在る。
== アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩で約15分。 * [[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩で約15分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|qq=}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|qq=}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|qq=|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|qq=|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.208861|N|136.985603|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460703]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Obata Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Obata Chausuyama kofun.jpg|250px]] |caption = Cross-section of the remaining rear circular section |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Suishōen 1-chome |coordinates = {{coord|35|12|31.90|N|136|59|08.17|E}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Front section destroyed) |map_size = Total length: 63m |built = Mid [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deceased = |excavations = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], weapons, horse trappings, ornaments |Historic Site Historic Site = |notes = }}
'''Obata Chausuyama Kofun''' (小幡茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Locally, it is often referred to simply as "Chausuyama Kofun."
== Overview ==
This kofun is one of the "Obata Kofun Cluster" (小幡古墳群), located around the Obata Plains. It was originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the front section was destroyed during the 1960s due to soil extraction and the construction of a road leading to {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|ja|愛知県立緑丘高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|1=Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494295}} (typeerly Aichi Prefectural Midorigaoka Commercial High School). Today, only part of the rear circular section remains. Despite its historical significance, the site has seen further development, with buildings constructed nearby, giving the mound the appearance of a mere cliff. Historical records also indicate the kofun was looted during the Taisho period.
== area and burial ==
A 1960 (Showa 35) survey recorded the kofun's total length as 63 meters, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 40 meters and the front section a width of 35 meters. During excavations from 1988 (Showa 63) to 1989 (Heisei 1), the remaining rear section was re-measured, revealing a diameter of 32 meters and a surrounding moat width of 6 meters. These excavations also uncovered a {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} on the southeast side of the rear section, along with multiple burial goods. The primary axis of the kofun was confirmed to point 55° east, and a preserved protruding "construction area" (造り出し) was found on the southern side of the constricted area.
== burial Chamber ==
The burial chamber is a left-side offset horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 8.2 meters in total length, with the burial chamber itself measuring 4.8 meters in length. Part of the burial chamber's floor remains, with 5–10 cm rounded pebbles laid on the surface. Processed stones in the sealing area suggest the possibility of secondary burials. A drainage ditch was also identified. The burial chamber was destroyed after the excavation.
== Excavated Items ==
burial goods discovered during the excavation are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}. Anecdotes from the Taisho period suggest "golden items" were found during looting, though details remain unclear.
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]][[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== External Links == * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama8.html Walking in Nagoya - Moriyama Ward, Obata]

{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Weapons: ** Iron swords (鉄刀) ** Iron arrowheads (鉄鏃) ** Small knives (刀子) ** {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} fragments * Horse trappings: ** Decorative fittings (辻金具) ** Bells (馬鈴) ** Stirrups (鐙) * Ornaments: ** Gold rings (金環) ** Silver rings (銀環) ** Beads (玉) }}
== References == * ''Moriyama no Kofun'' (守山の古墳) - Moriyama City Education Committee, 1963 * ''Obata Chausuyama Kofun Excavation Report'' (守山区小幡 茶臼山古墳 発掘調査報告書) - Nagoya City Miharadai Archaeological Museum, Nagoya City Education Committee, 1990 * ''Aichi Prefecture History: Materials Vol. 3, Kofun'' (愛知県史 資料編3 古墳) - Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee, Aichi Prefecture, 2004 * ''New Nagoya City History: Materials, Archaeology Vol. 1'' (新修名古屋市史 資料編 考古1) - New Nagoya City History Editorial Committee, Nagoya City, 2008== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|ja|小幡長塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obata Nagatsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun in the Obata Kofun Cluster, located less than a 10-minute walk away.
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu}}{{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Station (Aichi)|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419124}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus}} - Approximately a 15-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
